candidates.why: Represents the company’s total top-line revenue; explicitly labeled and aggregates all segment revenues.
This is a cost line (cost of revenues), not revenue; inappropriate for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Accrued voyage expenses and unearned revenue
candidates.why: Balance sheet liability related to timing of revenue recognition, not the income statement revenue figure.

variables.notes: Use Total Revenue as the primary REVT line; other candidates are not top-line revenue.

candidates.why: Explicit cost of revenues / COGS line; directly matches definition of COGS.
Inventory component on balance sheet, not period expense COGS.
Another inventory component on balance sheet; not the COGS expense line.

variables.notes: Income statement Cost of revenues/COGS is the correct production cost measure.

candidates.why: Explicit SG&A-type operating overhead bucket with substantial values; matches SG&A definition best.
Mixed operating income/expense bucket and appears to be more of a residual; less clearly SG&A than General & Admin.

variables.notes: Rule (a): explicit SG&A-type row exists and is populated, so only select 'General & Admin' for XSGA_COMPONENTS.
No R&D-related row is present among candidates; treat XRD as missing (0) for PROF.

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line on income statement; best match for net interest cost (before any capitalization).
Label suggests some interest-related adjustment, but meaning is unclear; secondary fallback only.
Represents profit attributable to minority shareholders, not interest on debt; should not be used for XINT.
Same as non-controlling interests; equity attribution, not interest expense.
Interest costs capitalized to assets, not expensed; could be relevant as a fallback interest component if main interest expense were missing.
Balance sheet equity item, not an interest expense line.
Balance sheet supplemental item with no amounts shown; if used at all, only as a very low-priority fallback.

variables.notes: Use a priority list for XINT. Main line is Interest expense; if missing for some years, fall back to Interest Capitalized, then Interest Dilution, then Balance Sheet Interest. Exclude minority interest lines as they are equity flows, not interest.

candidates.why: Income/valuation line related to an investment in an associate, not total shareholders’ equity.
Represents net income attributable to equity holders, not the balance sheet equity stock.
Component of equity (e.g., OCI), not total equity; should not be used alone as BE.
Explicit total shareholders’ equity line; best representation of book equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to equity holders of SNL
candidates.why: Equity attributable to parent shareholders; very close to total equity but excludes minority interests; good secondary choice.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to equity holders of SNL

variables.notes: Use Total Shareholders Equity as primary BE; Equity attributable to equity holders of SNL as secondary if the total line is unavailable.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to non-controlling interests; not the balance sheet minority equity but related conceptually.
Duplicate label of non-controlling interests on income statement; also a flow, not stock.
Balance sheet minority interest / non-controlling interests; this is the standard book value measure for MIB.

variables.notes: Select the balance sheet Minority Interest as MIB; it represents the stock of non-controlling interests. Income statement lines are flows and not appropriate for BE-style MIB.
